# 10 Most Common Customer Complaints and Their Solutions

No matter the industry, customer complaints are a reality of doing business. Complaints highlight areas where expectations aren’t being met, whether a delayed response, a billing error, or a frustrating return policy.

This is especially true in high-touch industries like banking, healthcare, and retail, where customer interactions are frequent and service quality matters most.

While complaints can feel like setbacks, they’re actually valuable insights. They reveal gaps in service, operational inefficiencies, and opportunities for improvement. [...] ## Why Listening to Customer Complaints Matters

Customer complaints are more than just frustrations. They provide valuable insights into service gaps, friction points, and unmet expectations. Yet, many businesses fail to recognize their importance.

Research shows that 96% of unhappy customers don’t complain, but 91% of them will leave without saying a word. This means businesses often lose customers without knowing why.

Frequent customer complaints act as early warning signals, helping businesses identify issues before they escalate.

When handled correctly, they can reduce churn, improve customer satisfaction, and strengthen brand loyalty. A well-resolved complaint can turn a frustrated customer into a long-term advocate. [...] ### 5. Repetitive Information Requests

Customers often find it frustrating to repeatedly provide the same personal information, account details, or case history. This is especially common in healthcare, banking, and customer support interactions, where inefficient data systems lead to unnecessary repetition.

To solve this issue, businesses can integrate self-check-in kiosks and CRM systems that allow customers to pre-fill or verify their details once, eliminating the need to repeat information.

For example, many healthcare facilities now use kiosks where patients can scan their IDs to check in rather than filling out paper forms multiple times. This speeds up the process, reduces frustration, and enhances the customer experience.

results.title: Customer Complaints: 8 Common Complaints & How to Resolve Them
results.content:
### 5. Poor Product or Service

When your product breaks, you can expect the customer to complain. In some cases, the product isn't broken, rather, the customer doesn't understand how to use it. Other times, customers aren't a good fit for your product or service, but they blame your company for failing to fulfill their needs. No matter how customers arrive at this conclusion, your team needs to know how to prevent them from turning to your competitors.

.png)

#### How to resolve this customer complaint: [...] If you know that their problem is one that has no solution due to limitations of your system or some other reason, acknowledge their frustration and detail what actions you're taking based on their feedback (forwarding their concerns to the appropriate department, escalating the ticket, etc.).

## Strive to Delight Customers

In the end, not all customer complaints will be resolved to the customer's satisfaction, and some customers may still walk away upset. However, it's up to you to provide a great experience to reduce these instances where you can.

Editor's note: This post was originally published in July 2019 and has been updated for comprehensiveness. [...] #### How to resolve this customer complaint:

If the product isn't broken, educate the customer. Find out what their goals and needs are, then teach them how they can use the product to achieve success. If the product is broken, provide options for immediate replacement and try to determine how it broke. If it was user error, gently point out to the customer how they can avoid this outcome in the future.

For a long-term solution, consider adopting customer feedback tools to survey customers about your product. You can use NPS® surveys to measure customer satisfaction and learn how you can enhance your product's features. These feedback tools provide both quantitative and qualitative data that you can use to improve product development.

### 6. No First Call Resolution

results.title: Como lidar com clientes irritados
results.content:
Entendo como essa situação foi frustrante, por isso, estamos facilitando ao máximo a devolução do item incorreto. Peço desculpas por adicionar essa etapa extra.

Aqui estão as instruções de devolução:

Se uma etiqueta de devolução pré-paga não for encontrada dentro da caixa, clique neste link para imprimir o formulário.

Mais uma vez, [Customer Name], peço sinceras desculpas pelo erro e pelo incômodo que ele causou. Agradeço sua paciência e assistência.

Atenciosamente,  
 [Agent Name]

### 4. Modelo de e-mail de dificuldades técnicas

As empresas de tecnologia e os provedores de serviços devem se desculpar quando ocorrem serviços irregulares ou problemas de back-end. É importante explicar o que deu errado e tentar compensar a dor de cabeça que isso causou.

Prezado(a) [Customer Name], [...] Prezado(a) [Customer Name],

Peço sinceras desculpas pela frustração que esses problemas devem ter causado. Para compensar você, reembolsei sua taxa de assinatura deste mês.

Parece que os problemas que você teve foram resultado de [explanation]. Identificamos a origem do problema e estamos nos empenhando em implementar uma correção o mais rápido possível. Tudo deve ser resolvido até [expected time]. Assim que o acesso for restabelecido, entrarei em contato e lhe informarei.

Mais uma vez, peço desculpas pelo incômodo causado. Não hesite em entrar em contato comigo diretamente se precisar de assistência adicional.

Atenciosamente,  
 [Agent Name]

### 5. Modelo de e-mail de resposta tardia [...] ### 2. Modelo de e-mail de pedido atrasado

Os clientes se acostumaram com o envio rápido. Portanto, quando um pedido não chega na data de entrega prometida, o cliente pode ficar frustrado. Isso se aplica particularmente aos itens com prazo, como presentes para a temporada de festas.

Embora seja melhor ser proativo e entrar em contato com o cliente para informá-lo do atraso antes da data de entrega prevista, talvez você não consiga contatar todos eles. Se um cliente irritado entrar em contato para reclamar de um pedido atrasado, rastreie o pacote e envie um e-mail explicando o status imediatamente.

Lamento muito que seu pedido ainda não tenha chegado. Entendo como isso deve ser frustrante para você.

results.title: Customer complaints: Top 7 common complaints & how to resolve them | Zoom
results.content:
Staff confusion

When customers reach out for support, they become flustered if they reach a call center agent who can only provide scripted responses that don’t resolve their problem. They’re also frustrated if they’ve previously talked to another customer support agent, their new rep doesn’t have the context of the situation, and they need to start from scratch. Make sure that your customer support tools can gather the context of your customer’s problem in advance so you know exactly where to route him for help. [...] If you run a restaurant, you can generally tell who your unhappy customers are. They’ll be the ones scowling at their overcooked food or glaring at their phones while waiting too long for a dish. If your servers pay close attention, ask for feedback often, and work to make problems right, they should be able to turn negative experiences into positive ones. They can also avoid frustrated diners turning to Yelp to write one-star reviews or blasting your brand on social media with posts filled with customer complaints. [...] #### Complicated interactive voice response (IVR) system

Customers can quickly become frustrated if they can’t access the right department with the choices offered on the interactive voice response (IVR) system or have no option to talk to a live customer support agent. A survey conducted by CSG found that 36% of respondents would rather wait on hold to speak with a human agent than use an AI-powered virtual assistant to resolve their issue.
